name: Sierra de Enmedio y Moclín - circular from Pinos Puente
name_original: Sierra de Enmedio y Moclín - circular desde Pinos Puente
region_intro: Road cycling around Pinos Puente offers diverse terrain, from the fertile plains of the Vega de Granada to the rolling hills dotted with olive groves. The region is situated on the western slope of Sierra Elvira, providing access to routes with panoramic views. Cyclists can explore routes along the River Cubillas and its reservoir, or venture into more challenging climbs in the surrounding areas. This varied landscape provides options for different cycling preferences and fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around Pinos Puente The most popular road cycling route is Sierra de Enmedio y Moclín - circular from Pinos Puente, a 33.2 miles (53.5 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a circular journey through the Sierra de Enmedio and Moclín mountains. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Spring water source in Olivares – Cubillas Reservoir loop from Fuente Vaqueros, a moderate 31.1 miles (50.1 km) path. This loop features scenic views around the Cubillas Reservoir and passes by a spring water source. Local road cyclists also love the Cubillas Reservoir – Colomera Reservoir loop from Fuente Vaqueros, a 44.4 miles (71.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain connecting two significant reservoirs, often completed in about 3 hours 16 minutes. Road cycling around Pinos Puente is defined by the flatlands of the Vega de Granada, the rolling hills with olive groves, and routes offering views of the Sierra Elvira. The network provides options for easy rides along river plains and more challenging routes with elevation gain. The routes in Pinos Puente are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 290 reviews. More than 6,400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Pinos Puente's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Pinos Puente?
A: There are over 130 road cycling routes available around Pinos Puente, catering to various skill levels. This includes more than 30 easy routes, nearly 60 moderate routes, and 40 difficult routes for experienced cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Pinos Puente?
A: Pinos Puente offers a diverse range of terrain. You can expect flatlands through the fertile Vega de Granada, rolling hills dotted with extensive olive groves, and more challenging climbs on the western slopes of Sierra Elvira, providing panoramic views of Granada and the Sierra Nevada mountains.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Pinos Puente?
A: The region generally offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with pleasant temperatures. Summers can be hot, especially in the middle of the day, while winters are milder but can still be cool, particularly at higher elevations.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Pinos Puente?
A: Yes, Pinos Puente has over 30 easy road cycling routes. These often follow the flatlands of the Vega de Granada or gentle paths along the River Cubillas, offering a less strenuous experience. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31962071" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Fe – Path along the Genil River loop from Fuente Vaqueros&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 43.4 km route.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs near Pinos Puente?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Pinos Puente's proximity to the Sierra Nevada mountains provides access to demanding climbs. Routes venturing into the Sierra Elvira, such as those towards Monte El Piorno, will offer significant elevation gain and rewarding panoramic views. There are 40 difficult routes available in the area.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Pinos Puente?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in the Pinos Puente area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983603710/sierra-de-enmedio-y-moclin-circular-from-pinos-puente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Enmedio y Moclín - circular from Pinos Puente&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 53.5 km route through the mountains.

Q: What natural features or scenic viewpoints can I see along road cycling routes in Pinos Puente?
A: Road cycling routes often feature beautiful waterside scenery around the Embalse del Cubillas (Cubillas Reservoir) and views of the distant Sierra Nevada mountains. You'll also pass through lush poplar trees in the Vega de Granada and extensive olive groves in the rolling hills, providing a quintessential Spanish backdrop.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or cultural sites I can visit while cycling?
A: Absolutely. Cyclists can incorporate cultural exploration into their rides. Notable landmarks include the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3682350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinos Puente Bridge&lt;/a&gt; (Puente Califal), which has Roman and Moorish origins. The region also features archaeological sites like Cerro del Piorno y La Molaina, offering glimpses into rich history.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that pass by the Cubillas Reservoir?
A: Yes, the Cubillas Reservoir is a prominent feature in many road cycling routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39560302" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Spring water source in Olivares – Cubillas Reservoir loop from Fuente Vaqueros&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 50.1 km path that offers scenic views around the reservoir and passes by a spring water source.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Pinos Puente?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 290 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from flat plains to challenging climbs, and the beautiful scenery, including views of the Sierra Nevada and the Cubillas Reservoir.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer rides, routes connecting multiple reservoirs or exploring further into the surrounding countryside are available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38234740" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cubillas Reservoir – Colomera Reservoir loop from Fuente Vaqueros&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 71.6 km trail connecting two significant reservoirs.

Q: Are there specific routes that offer views of the Sierra Nevada mountains?
A: Many routes, particularly those on the western slope of Sierra Elvira or around the Embalse del Cubillas, offer impressive panoramic views of the majestic Sierra Nevada mountains in the distance, especially on clear days.
tips:
Many recreational activities such as windsurfing, canoeing and jet skiing are permitted on this lake. A large picnic area has been created on its north shore, once past the dam. In this area you will also find a restaurant. This body of water allows you to take very beautiful photos, especially on a windless day when the Sierra Nevada is covered with snow.
The Cubillas Reservoir is one of the main bird watching areas in the province of Granada, as it serves as a transit station for migrations and a nesting space for some species.

Visiting Moclín is usually a highly recommended experience, especially if you enjoy destinations that combine history, nature, and tranquility.

The best of Moclín:
Impressive historical heritage: The Nasrid castle and church are true gems for lovers of medieval history.
Spectacular natural surroundings: The Gollizno Route is ideal for hiking, with suspension bridges and viewpoints overlooking the valley.
Charming villages: Cobblestone streets, whitewashed houses, and photogenic corners.
Authentic experience: It's not a crowded destination, allowing you to enjoy it at a leisurely pace.

waypoints.name: View of Moclín and Moclín Castle
